title: chore: parallel deployment of enter+gen on a separate CF account
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Mirrors enter+gen workers onto a separate Cloudflare account for an isolated parallel deployment. Pollinations stack on main is untouched.

## Changes
- Wrangler configs (enter + gen) repoint account / D1 / KV / R2 / routes to the other CF account
- Client `config.ts` and a few hardcoded URLs (Stripe checkout image, OAuth modal links) updated to the new hostnames
- Canonical link and `response_format=url` image URL are now derived dynamically from the request origin instead of hardcoded
- `scripts/cf-account.sh` switches the local wrangler default between the two account profiles
- Encrypted sops bundle updated with new GitHub OAuth client credentials for the new app
- Test expectation updated to match the new Stripe checkout image hostname

## Test plan
- [ ] Sign-in flow on the new host reaches GitHub OAuth consent
- [ ] Image gen via `gptimage` returns a 1024×1024 image with an imported API key
- [ ] Text gen via `/v1/chat/completions` (`openai-fast`) returns expected content
- [ ] `npm run test` in `enter.pollinations.ai` (one pre-existing unrelated failure on date-bounds test)
- [ ] No plaintext secrets in the diff (sops fields all `ENC[AES256_GCM,...]`)

gist.headline: Spawning a parallel hive
gist.blurb: We're setting up a parallel deployment of our core infrastructure on a separate Cloudflare account. Just some behind-the-scenes gardening to keep the ecosystem resilient!
gist.summary: Configured a parallel deployment of the enter and gen workers on a separate Cloudflare account, including dynamic origin routing and updated Wrangler configurations.
gist.impact: This isolated environment allows us to test infrastructure migrations safely without affecting the main production stack.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot setting up a second, identical server rack or garden bed next to the main one, representing a parallel deployment.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, working alongside a chunky monitor-headed robot to plant a second identical row of glowing lime green (#ecf874) code-plants in a digital garden. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
